_comment: Immutable scoring config for the core-api.yaml loop. The agent must NEVER edit this. score(spec) = (control_violations + arch_violations) * big_penalty + complexity. Keep iff score(candidate) < score(best): feasibility first (drive violations down), then minimize complexity.

weights._comment: Complexity weights. concept is heaviest so deleting/merging concepts pays most.

_enum_note: weights.enum is charged per enum value above enum_cap, summed across every enum in the doc. Closes a blind spot: enum VALUES are schema-internal constraints the parser never lifts into the vocabulary, so without this term a 337-value Task.type cost zero and the minimizer could neither see nor delete it. Cap 12 leaves normal status/type enums free; a 337-value enum contributes 325, making its collapse to the ~28 generic verbs a large, keep-worthy score win. The companion 'unused supply' signal (control_oracle: fields/events no control cites) is reported in score.coverage as deletion candidates for the proposer, but is intentionally NOT a score gate — much uncited supply is architecture-required or contract plumbing, and the arch gate already protects load-bearing elements.
_money_float_note: Penalty per money amount typed as float (`number`). Money must be integer minor units in a ledger system — float rounding drift breaks Sum(balances)==settlement invariants invisibly. Ratios/scores/rates legitimately stay number (see fitness.MONEY_WORDS / NONMONEY_WORDS). Weighted above `field` so converting amounts to integer is a score-improving move the loop keeps.
_global_dl_note: Off by default (API-only fitness, cheap). Set true once the outer loop lands to also penalize pushing complexity from the spec into controls.json / control-vocabulary.json (byte-length term).
_control_budget_note: Allowed unregistered codes before it counts as a violation. control_violations = max(0, unregistered - control_budget). Default 0 = hard coverage (every cited code must be registered). The user's chosen default is a PROVISIONAL budget: set this to the current measured baseline (live full-repo = 1116; a fair-lending-scoped phase-1 run will be far smaller) so the loop starts in the minimize-complexity regime instead of having to register every code first. Tighten toward 0 as coverage improves. The keep-iff-best rule has teeth at any budget because it is relative: any move that raises unregistered raises the score.
_arch_budget_note: Allowed uncovered architectural elements before it counts as a violation. arch_violations = max(0, uncovered - arch_budget). Default 0 = hard architecture coverage. Current measured baseline uncovered = 56 with endpoint_derivation='stateful' (the missing banking-core endpoints + per-resource REST the derivation now requires); ~186 with 'all'. Endpoints are now DERIVED from resources (see architecture-spec.json endpoint_derivation), so resource-backed endpoints like /cases are load-bearing and cannot be deleted for free. Set a provisional arch_budget if you want to minimize complexity before building out endpoints.
